description: This figure presents Thermo-Calc calculated thermodynamic phase fractions of Zr0.50 steel (Fe-25Mn-7Al-0.9C-0.50Zr low-density steel) as a function of temperature. The left subplot covers the temperature range of 500℃ to 1000℃: FCC phase (red curve) rises from ~0.65 at 500℃ to nearly 1.0 at 700℃ and remains stable at higher temperatures; BCC phase (blue curve) and κ carbide (purple curve) decrease from low initial values at 500℃ to 0 around 700℃. The right subplot shows low-magnitude phase fractions, where ZrC (green curve) maintains a stable value of ~0.0058 (~0.6%) above 900℃, while BCC phase fraction is almost zero. These results verify that sufficient ZrC precipitates exist at the 900℃ annealing temperature, which provides Zener pinning effect to inhibit grain boundary migration during recrystallization, supporting the formation mechanism of heterogeneous grain structure in the studied Zr-alloyed low-density steel.
